provenance.source_corpus: A scientific basis for restoring fish spawning habitat in the St. Clair and Detroit Rivers of the Laurentian Great Lakes

nodes.name: Siltation and removal of fish spawning habitats, altered hydrological conditions disconnecting floodplains from river channels, and water pollution

source_spans.text: Siltation and removal of fish spawning habitats, altered hydrological conditions disconnecting floodplains from river channels, and water pollution have reduced the reproductive capacity of large river systems

Siltation and removal of fish spawning habitats, altered hydrological conditions disconnecting floodplains from river channels, and water pollution have reduced the reproductive capacity of large river systems

nodes.name: overfishing and the removal of more than 46 million m3 of substrates

source_spans.text: By 1925, overfishing and the removal of more than 46 million m3 of substrates from the Detroit River... and a like amount of substrates from the St. Clair River... greatly reduced the amount of spawning habitat and fish populations in these rivers.

By 1925, overfishing and the removal of more than 46 million m3 of substrates from the Detroit River... and a like amount of substrates from the St. Clair River... greatly reduced the amount of spawning habitat and fish populations in these rivers.
Particularly damaging was the Livingstone Channel project... destroyed many hectares of fish spawning grounds
Particularly damaging was the Livingstone Channel project... destroyed many hectares of fish spawning grounds
Using an adaptive management approach, we created 1,100m2 of productive fish spawning habitat
Using an adaptive management approach, we created 1,100m2 of productive fish spawning habitat
Post-construction fisheries assessments conducted since 2004 have revealed that 14 species of fish... spawned on the man-made reefs at Belle Isle
Post-construction fisheries assessments conducted since 2004 have revealed that 14 species of fish... spawned on the man-made reefs at Belle Isle
These reefs were promptly used for reproduction by A. fulvescens, S. vitreus, and C. clupeaformis

nodes.name: reproduction by A. fulvescens, S. vitreus, and C. clupeaformis

source_spans.text: These reefs were promptly used for reproduction by A. fulvescens, S. vitreus, and C. clupeaformis
Spawning habitat on the west side of the channel, where velocities were faster (>0.7msec−1), remained viable with no evidence of infilling.
Spawning habitat on the west side of the channel, where velocities were faster (>0.7msec−1), remained viable with no evidence of infilling.
small differences in reef placement within a channel can influence infilling with sediment and reef performance.
small differences in reef placement within a channel can influence infilling with sediment and reef performance.
Sites selected for future reef restoration have lower potential for infilling, due to the team’s ability to identify and avoid depositional zones
Sites selected for future reef restoration have lower potential for infilling, due to the team’s ability to identify and avoid depositional zones
